title: Bug Bounty Platform Orientation
when_to_use: When preparing to participate in bug bounty programmes

steps.title: Create Accounts
steps.instruction: Create accounts on HackerOne and Bugcrowd
steps.tip: Use a professional username. Your bug bounty profile becomes part of your professional reputation

steps.title: Read Programme Policies
steps.instruction: Read 3 different programme policies (scope, rules, rewards)
steps.tip: Pay special attention to: what's in scope, what testing methods are prohibited, and reward structures

steps.title: Note Differences
steps.instruction: Note the differences: some allow automated scanning, some do not
steps.tip: Violating programme rules can get you banned from the platform entirely. Read carefully

steps.title: Read Disclosed Reports
steps.instruction: Read 3 disclosed reports to understand good report format
steps.tip: Look for reports with high signal ratings. Note the structure, detail level, and communication style

steps.title: Identify Beginner Programme
steps.instruction: Identify a programme with a wide scope for beginners
steps.tip: Look for programmes marked 'beginner friendly' or with wide web application scope. Avoid narrow scopes initially

steps.title: Bookmark Scope
steps.instruction: Bookmark the programme's scope page for reference
steps.tip: Check scope before every testing session. Programmes update their scope regularly

steps.title: Scope Compliance
steps.instruction: Never test outside the defined scope of any programme
steps.tip: Out-of-scope testing is unauthorised access. It can result in legal action, not just platform ban
